homepage.heroSubtitle: Hop-on, hop-off day pass with guaranteed seating and professional local guides. Unlimited rides in small tuk tuks and vintage jeeps. New departure every 30 minutes.
homepage.sectionOneDescription: Skip the crowded tourist buses and experience Sintra the way it was meant to be discovered—with professional local guides
homepage.serviceDescription: Hop On Sintra is a flexible hop-on/hop-off day pass service using small, intimate vehicles like tuk tuks and vintage UMM jeeps. With service every 30 minutes from 9am-7pm, you get guaranteed seating and unlimited rides to all major attractions.
homepage.serviceDescription2: Unlike crowded tour buses, our small groups (2-6 passengers) with professional local guides give you the freedom to explore Sintra at your own pace while enjoying personalized attention.

about.title: About Hop On Sintra
about.subtitle: Your premium hop-on/hop-off day pass service through Sintra's magical landscapes with professional local guides
story: Hop On Sintra was born from a simple observation: visitors to this UNESCO World Heritage site deserved better than crowded buses and rigid schedules.
We created a flexible, premium day pass service that combines the intimacy of small vehicles driven by professional local guides with the freedom of hop-on/hop-off convenience.

Today, thousands of visitors choose Hop On Sintra for guaranteed seating, professional driver-guides, regular departures every 30 minutes, and an authentic, intimate way to explore this magical destination.
about.mission: Our mission is to provide the most convenient, comfortable, and authentic way to explore Sintra's palaces, castles, and gardens with professional local guides.

values.title: Guaranteed Comfort
values.description: Every passenger has a guaranteed seat in a small, intimate vehicle driven by a professional local guide. No standing, no overcrowding.

values.title: Total Flexibility
values.description: Hop off at any attraction, spend as long as you want, then hop back on. Your day, your pace.

values.title: Professional Local Guides
values.description: All our vehicles are driven by professional local guides who know Sintra inside and out, offering insights you won't find in guidebooks.

about.storyHeading: How Hop On Sintra Was Born

about.valuesSubtitle: Every decision we make is guided by these principles
about.faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about Hop On Sintra

faq.q: What's included in the day pass?
faq.a: Unlimited rides on all participating vehicles (tuk-tuks, UMM jeeps) with professional driver-guides for one full day. Guaranteed seating in every vehicle. You can optionally add attraction entrance tickets.

faq.q: Can I buy attraction tickets with my pass?
faq.a: Yes! During booking you can add entrance tickets to popular attractions like Pena Palace, Quinta da Regaleira, and more. This saves you time—no waiting in ticket lines.
Our 10 AM and 2 PM departures offer guided tours where your professional driver-guide stops at key attractions to share expert commentary about Sintra's history and hidden gems.

faq.q: How do I use my QR code?
faq.a: Simply show your QR code (sent via email) to any Hop On Sintra driver before boarding. They'll scan it to verify your pass.
Vehicles depart every 30 minutes from all major attractions during operating hours (9:00 AM - 7:00 PM).

pena-palace.name: Pena Palace
pena-palace.description: The crown jewel of Sintra, this Romanticist palace sits atop a hill with stunning views. Built in the 19th century, it showcases vibrant colors and eclectic architecture.
pena-palace.longDescription: Built on the ruins of an old monastery, Pena Palace was commissioned by King Ferdinand II and represents the peak of Romantic architecture in Portugal. The palace combines Gothic, Moorish, Renaissance, and Manueline styles in a whimsical display of color and creativity. The surrounding park features exotic trees and plants from around the world, winding paths, and breathtaking viewpoints.
highlights: Panoramic views of Sintra and the Atlantic Ocean
Ornate interior rooms with original 19th-century furnishings
Exotic botanical gardens with over 500 tree species
tips: Arrive early to avoid crowds, especially in summer
Wear comfortable shoes - lots of walking and hills
Don't miss the palace terrace for the best views
Visit the park before or after touring the palace

quinta-regaleira.name: Quinta da Regaleira
quinta-regaleira.description: A mystical estate featuring enigmatic gardens, underground tunnels, and the famous Initiation Well. This UNESCO World Heritage site is a masterpiece of symbolic and philosophical architecture.
quinta-regaleira.longDescription: Created by Italian architect Luigi Manini for millionaire António Augusto Carvalho Monteiro, Quinta da Regaleira is filled with alchemical and masonic symbolism. The estate features elaborate gardens, mysterious grottoes, enchanting lakes, and the iconic Initiation Well - a 27-meter spiral staircase descending into the earth, used for ceremonial purposes.
highlights: The famous Initiation Well with spiral staircase
tips: Bring a flashlight for exploring the tunnels
Wear sturdy shoes - paths can be slippery
Allow time to explore all the hidden corners
The Initiation Well is a must-see photo spot

moorish-castle.name: Moorish Castle
moorish-castle.description: Ancient fortress walls snake along mountain ridges, offering spectacular panoramic views. Built by the Moors in the 8th-9th centuries, this castle is a testament to medieval military architecture.
moorish-castle.longDescription: The Moorish Castle was built during the Muslim occupation of the Iberian Peninsula, strategically positioned to guard the town of Sintra and surrounding trade routes. After the Christian conquest, the castle fell into disrepair but was later restored in the 19th century. Today, visitors can walk along the ancient ramparts and enjoy some of the most spectacular views in the region.
highlights: Scenic hiking trails through the forest
Stunning photo opportunities at every turn
tips: Excellent workout - lots of stairs and climbing
Best visited in the morning for clearer views
Combine with Pena Palace for a full day
Bring water and sun protection

monserrate-palace.name: Monserrate Palace
monserrate-palace.description: An exotic palace blending Gothic, Indian, and Moorish influences, set within one of the most beautiful botanical gardens in Portugal with rare and exotic plant species from around the world.
monserrate-palace.longDescription: Monserrate Palace was built in 1858 for Sir Francis Cook, an English textile magnate and art collector. The palace showcases an extraordinary fusion of architectural styles, while its gardens are considered among the finest examples of landscape gardening in Portugal. The estate features plants from five continents, creating microclimates that support species from Mexico, Australia, Japan, and beyond.
tips: Often less crowded than other palaces
Don't rush - the gardens deserve time
Perfect for photography enthusiasts
Bring a picnic to enjoy in the gardens

sintra-palace.name: Sintra National Palace
sintra-palace.description: Located in the heart of Sintra's historic center, this is the best-preserved medieval royal palace in Portugal, famous for its distinctive twin chimneys and magnificent tile work.
sintra-palace.longDescription: The Sintra National Palace has been a royal residence since the early 15th century and was continuously inhabited by Portuguese royalty until 1910. The palace showcases various architectural styles accumulated over the centuries, from medieval to Renaissance. Its interior features some of the finest examples of Mudéjar tilework in Portugal, along with painted ceilings depicting armillary spheres, magpies, and swans.
highlights: Historic Sala dos Brasões (Coat of Arms Room)
tips: Start or end your day here - it's in town center
Audio guide recommended for full historical context
Great for a rainy day - mostly indoor
Combine with exploring Sintra's cafes and shops

convento-capuchos.name: Convento dos Capuchos
convento-capuchos.description: A serene 16th-century Franciscan monastery built into natural rock formations, offering a unique glimpse into monastic life of simplicity and communion with nature.
convento-capuchos.longDescription: The Convent of the Capuchin Friars was founded in 1560 and is one of the most remarkable examples of monastic architecture in Portugal. Built directly into massive boulders and cork trees, the convent embodies the Franciscan ideals of poverty and harmony with nature. The tiny cells, low doorways, and cork-lined walls create an atmosphere of profound tranquility and humility. Abandoned in 1834, the convent has been preserved as a monument to simple living.
highlights: Unique architecture integrated with natural rock
Peaceful forest setting away from crowds
Tiny monks' cells carved into boulders
Stunning views over the Sintra mountains
tips: Be prepared for low doorways - watch your head!
Wear comfortable shoes for uneven paths
Bring water - limited facilities on site
Perfect for those seeking peace and quiet

cabo-da-roca.name: Cabo da Roca
cabo-da-roca.description: The westernmost point of mainland Europe, where dramatic cliffs meet the Atlantic Ocean. Experience breathtaking coastal views and stand at the edge of the continent.
cabo-da-roca.longDescription: Cabo da Roca is located at latitude 38° 47' north and longitude 9° 30' west, marking the westernmost extent of the European mainland. The cape features a lighthouse built in 1772 and offers spectacular views of the Atlantic Ocean crashing against 140-meter-high cliffs. Portuguese poet Luís de Camões described it as 'where the land ends and the sea begins.' The dramatic landscape and powerful ocean create an unforgettable experience.
highlights: Certificate of visit available at gift shop
cabo-da-roca.hours: Open 24 hours (Lighthouse and shop: 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM)
tips: Can be very windy - bring a jacket
Best visited at sunset for stunning photos
Free to visit - no entrance fee
Get your certificate to prove you've been

villa-sassetti.name: Villa Sassetti
villa-sassetti.description: An elegant Italian-style villa surrounded by romantic gardens, connected to the historic center by a scenic pedestrian trail through lush woodland.
villa-sassetti.longDescription: Villa Sassetti was built in 1890 by Italian merchant Victor Carlos Sassetti, designed by Luigi Manini (the same architect behind Quinta da Regaleira). The villa showcases Italian architectural influences and sits within beautifully landscaped gardens featuring exotic trees, ornamental fountains, and romantic pathways. The property is connected to Sintra's historic center by a picturesque walking trail that winds through the forest, offering a peaceful escape from the busier attractions.
highlights: Scenic forest trail to Sintra center
tips: Free admission to the gardens
Perfect spot for a peaceful walk
Combine with the walking trail to town
Great for avoiding tourist crowds

biester-chalet.name: Biester Chalet
biester-chalet.description: A charming romantic-era chalet hidden in Sintra's hills, showcasing elegant architecture and beautiful gardens. This lesser-known gem offers an intimate glimpse into 19th-century aristocratic life.
biester-chalet.longDescription: Built in the 1890s for Ernest Biester, a wealthy businessman and art collector, the Biester Chalet represents the romantic eclecticism of late 19th-century architecture. Designed by French architect José Leopoldo Gouveia, the chalet combines Swiss chalet influences with Portuguese decorative elements. After extensive restoration, the property opened to visitors, revealing ornate interiors with original frescoes, carved woodwork, and period furnishings set within beautifully landscaped gardens.
tips: Book tickets in advance - limited daily capacity
Guided tours provide fascinating historical context
Perfect for architecture and design enthusiasts
Combine with nearby Seteais Palace gardens

queluz-palace.name: Palace of Queluz
queluz-palace.description: Often called the 'Portuguese Versailles', this magnificent 18th-century palace features opulent rococo interiors and stunning French-inspired gardens.
queluz-palace.longDescription: The Palace of Queluz was built as a summer retreat for Dom Pedro of Braganza, later King Pedro III and husband to Queen Maria I. Designed by architects Mateus Vicente de Oliveira and Jean-Baptiste Robillon, the palace exemplifies Portuguese rococo architecture at its finest. The lavish interiors feature gilded halls, painted ceilings, and ornate decorations, while the geometric gardens are adorned with statues, fountains, and topiary in the French formal style.
highlights: Stunning formal gardens inspired by Versailles
Throne Room with crystal chandeliers and mirrors
Azulejo canal with decorative tile panels
Royal ceremonial halls and private chambers
tips: Located 15km from Sintra center - plan transportation
Gardens are spectacular in spring and summer
Less crowded than Sintra's main attractions
Combined tickets available with Sintra National Palace

mafra-convent.name: Mafra National Palace and Convent
mafra-convent.description: One of Europe's most impressive baroque monuments, this colossal palace-convent complex features a magnificent library, ornate basilica, and royal apartments. A UNESCO World Heritage masterpiece.
mafra-convent.longDescription: Commissioned by King João V in 1717 to fulfill a vow made for an heir, the Mafra National Palace represents the wealth and power of 18th-century Portugal. This monumental complex combines a royal palace, Franciscan convent, basilica, and one of Europe's most beautiful baroque libraries. Construction took 13 years and employed over 50,000 workers. The building's scale is staggering - it contains 1,200 rooms, 4,700 doors and windows, and 156 stairways. The rocaille library houses over 36,000 leather-bound volumes.
highlights: Impressive basilica with 6 organs and 2 bell towers
Vast monastery with hospital and pharmacy
tips: Located 40km from Sintra - plan for travel time
Guided tours highly recommended for full experience
Photography not allowed in the library
One of Portugal's most important baroque monuments

sintraTrainStation.name: Sintra Train Station
sintraTrainStation.info: Located in the heart of Sintra, just steps from the historic center. Our vehicles pick up right outside the main station entrance. Connection point for both routes.

historicalCenterNorth.name: Historical Center North
historicalCenterNorth.description: Historic center with cafés and shops
historicalCenterNorth.info: Pick up in the charming historic center, surrounded by local restaurants, pastry shops, and boutiques. Connection point for both routes.

historicalCenterSouth.name: Historical Center South
historicalCenterSouth.info: Stop in the historic center near the iconic National Palace with its distinctive twin chimneys.

moorishCastle.name: Moorish Castle
moorishCastle.info: Visit this 10th-century Moorish fortification perched on the hilltop, offering spectacular views over the region.

penaPalace.name: Pena Palace
penaPalace.description: Iconic hilltop castle with panoramic views
penaPalace.info: Visit Sintra's most famous landmark. Our pickup point is conveniently located at the palace entrance area.

quintaRegaleira.name: Quinta da Regaleira
quintaRegaleira.description: Mystical estate with initiation wells and caves
quintaRegaleira.info: Visit this enchanting estate featuring mysterious gardens, underground tunnels, and the famous Initiation Well. Pickup at the main entrance.

seteais.name: Seteais
seteais.info: Stop at this magnificent neoclassical palace with breathtaking views. Now a luxury hotel, the exterior and gardens are open to visitors.

monserratePalace.name: Monserrate Palace
monserratePalace.info: Pick up at this stunning 19th-century palace surrounded by botanical gardens featuring plants from around the world. A hidden gem of Sintra.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about our private tours
faq.question1: What's included in the tour price?
faq.answer1: All tours include a professional guide, private vehicle, hotel pickup and drop-off, bottled water, and insurance. Attraction entrance fees are not included.
faq.answer2: Absolutely! We can create a custom itinerary based on your interests, whether you want to focus on palaces, gardens, photography, or local culture.
faq.question3: What languages do the guides speak?
faq.answer3: All our guides are multilingual and speak English, Portuguese, Spanish, and French. Other languages available upon request.
faq.answer4: Tours operate rain or shine! We'll adjust the itinerary to focus on indoor attractions if needed, and provide umbrellas.
faq.question5: How far in advance should I book?
faq.answer5: We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during peak season (May-October). However, we'll do our best to accommodate last-minute requests.

ui.howItWorksSubtitle: Three simple steps to the best day of your trip! 🎉
ui.step1Description: Pick your date, add optional attractions, and boom—you're all set! Your digital pass arrives instantly via email. No printing, no hassle, just pure adventure.
ui.step2Description: Your smartphone becomes your ticket to Sintra! Save your QR code and you're ready to hop on at any of our stops. It's that simple.
ui.step3Description: See a tuk tuk at the stop? Flash your code to your professional driver-guide and jump in! With rides every 30 minutes from 9am to 7pm, you'll never wait long. Explore at your own pace—our guides have you covered all day long.
ui.step4Description: If you don't see any vehicles waiting when you arrive at a stop, you can request a pickup! This lets us know you're waiting and helps us get to you faster. Your request helps us optimize our service and reduce wait times for everyone.
ui.installAppDescription: Add to your home screen! Works offline, loads faster, and makes requesting pickups smoother. Takes just 2 seconds!

cookie.settingsDescription: Choose which cookies you want to accept. You can change your preferences at any time.

necessary.title: Necessary Cookies
necessary.description: Essential for the website to function properly. These cookies enable core functionality such as security, network management, and accessibility. You cannot opt-out of these cookies.

functional.title: Functional Cookies
functional.description: Help us remember your preferences and settings, such as language selection, to provide you with a more personalized experience.

analytics.title: Analytics Cookies
analytics.description: Help us understand how visitors interact with our website by collecting and reporting information anonymously. This helps us improve our service.

marketing.title: Marketing Cookies
marketing.description: Used to track visitors across websites to display relevant advertisements and encourage them to engage with our service.

liveChat.welcomeMessage: Hi! 👋 Welcome to Hop On Sintra. How can we help you today?

userProfile.loginDescription: Login with your booking ID and last name to access your temporary profile during your visit.
userProfile.pleaseEnterBoth: Please enter both booking ID and last name
userProfile.loginFailed: Failed to login. Please try again.

requestPickupPage.verifyBookingDescription: Enter your booking code to request a pickup. This service is only available to customers with active day passes.
requestPickupPage.requestPickupDescription: Tell us your group size and location, and we'll send the perfect vehicle for you.
requestPickupPage.pickupRequestedMessage: We've received your request and will dispatch a vehicle shortly.

bookingConfirmation.checkEmail: Check your email for confirmation and QR codes.
bookingConfirmation.saveForManaging: Save this for managing your booking
bookingConfirmation.ticketsSentToEmail: Your tickets PDF and booking details have been sent to your email.
bookingConfirmation.pdfTicketsAttachedDesc: We've sent a PDF with all your tickets to your email. You can also download it here or save your tickets below.
bookingConfirmation.ticketsReadyDesc: Show these tickets to the driver when boarding. Each passenger needs their own ticket with QR code.
bookingConfirmation.showQRCodeDesc: Present your QR code to the driver when boarding any vehicle
bookingConfirmation.unlimitedRidesDesc: Use your pass for unlimited hop-on/hop-off rides until 8:00 PM
bookingConfirmation.regularServiceDesc: New vehicles depart every 30 minutes from all major attractions
bookingConfirmation.flexibleScheduleDesc: Spend as much time as you want at each attraction
bookingConfirmation.thankYou: Thank You for Choosing Hop On Sintra!
bookingConfirmation.adventureBegins: We're excited to show you the magic of Sintra. Your adventure begins on
bookingConfirmation.confirmationSentTo: Your booking confirmation and tickets have been sent to
bookingConfirmation.tipUseBookingId: Use your Booking ID {bookingId} to manage your reservation
bookingConfirmation.questionsOrChanges: Questions or need to make changes?
bookingConfirmation.loginNow: Login now to access premium features during your visit:
bookingConfirmation.loginInstantly: Use your {bold}Booking ID{/bold} and {bold}last name{/bold} to login instantly.
bookingConfirmation.fullAccessEnabled: You have full access to all features including requesting pickups and live chat support. Your tickets and booking details are saved to your profile.
bookingConfirmation.noBookingFound: No booking found. Please make a booking first.
bookingConfirmation.lookForVehicle: to ensure a prompt departure. Look for our vehicle with the Hop On Sintra branding and show your ticket to the driver.

stepDescriptions.step1: Select your preferred date and start time
stepDescriptions.step2: Choose pickup location and number of guests

toasts.paymentInitFailed: Failed to initialize payment. Please try again.
toasts.bookingConfirmedCheckEmail: Booking confirmed! Check your email for QR codes.
toasts.bookingConfirmedQRReady: Booking confirmed! QR codes are ready.
toasts.emailVerificationWarning: ⚠️ Email system requires domain verification. QR codes are available on this page.
toasts.emailNoAddress: ⚠️ No email address provided. Save your QR codes from this page.
toasts.emailCouldntBeSent: Email couldn't be sent. Save your QR codes from this page.
toasts.serverConnectionIssue: Server connection issue. Your payment was processed. Please contact support with your payment confirmation.

bookingErrors.failedMultipleAttempts: Failed to create booking after multiple attempts
bookingErrors.failedToComplete: Failed to complete booking. Please try again.

insightTourInfo.title: Insight Tour
insightTourInfo.description: Select time slots include our Insight Tour, a longer and more detailed ride where the driver shares the stories and history behind Sintra's monuments. Look for time slots marked with the distinctive Insight Tour badge below.

soldOut.title: Temporarily Unavailable
soldOut.description: All dates are currently sold out. Please check back later or contact us for availability.

step2.title: Pickup & Group Size
step2.description: Choose your pickup location and number of guests

step3.title: Add Attraction Tickets?
step3.notAvailable: Attraction tickets are not yet available for online purchase. You can buy tickets at each attraction entrance.
step3.tipDescription: You'll receive digital tickets via email along with your

comingSoon.description: We're working on adding the ability to purchase attraction tickets online. For now, tickets can be purchased at each attraction entrance.
comingSoon.tip: 💡 Your Hop On Sintra day pass gets you unlimited transport to all attractions. Tickets are available for purchase when you arrive!

step4.title: Your Information
step4.qrCodeSentHere: Your QR code will be sent here

benefits.flexible: Flexible - use anytime during operating hours

aboutPage.messageSent: Thank you for your message! We'll get back to you soon.
aboutPage.messageError: Unable to send message. Please try WhatsApp or email us directly at info@hoponsintra.com

toast.newVersionAvailable: New version available! Refresh to update.
toast.settingsSaveFailed: Failed to save settings to database. Saved locally only.
toast.contentSaveFailed: Failed to save content. Please try again.

daypass.title: Full Day Pass
daypass.description: Unlimited rides across all Sintra attractions

insightTour.title: Insight Tour
insightTour.description: Deep dive into Sintra's history and culture

monuments.title: Monument Tickets
monuments.description: Skip the line at Sintra's top attractions

attractionDetail.ticketsAtEntrance: Attraction tickets are not yet available for online purchase. You can buy tickets at the entrance.
attractionDetail.dayPassPromo: Book a Hop On Sintra day pass for unlimited transport between all attractions with professional driver-guides!

privateTourDetail.freeCancellationDesc: Cancel up to 24 hours in advance for a full refund
privateTourDetail.privateExclusiveDesc: Exclusively for your group — no shared buses or strangers

tourBookingDialog.title: Book Your Private Tour
tourBookingDialog.pickDatePrompt: Pick a date to see available times
tourBookingDialog.minimumGuestsNotice: This tour has a {min}-guest minimum — that's what we'll charge for.
tourBookingDialog.languageNote: We guarantee tours in English, Spanish and Portuguese. For other languages we'll do our best to accommodate you!
tourBookingDialog.specialRequestsPlaceholder: Accessibility needs, dietary requirements, specific sites you'd love to visit…
tourBookingDialog.quoteInfo: We'll review your request and send you a personalised quote within 24 hours. No payment needed until you're happy to confirm.
tourBookingDialog.quoteSuccessMsg: We'll review your request and email you a personalised quote within 24 hours.
tourBookingDialog.bookingConfirmedSubtitle: Your adventure is locked in. We can't wait to show you Sintra!
tourBookingDialog.nextStep1Body: A full summary has been sent to your inbox.
tourBookingDialog.nextStep2Body: Your guide will reach out 24 hours before the tour with meeting point details.
tourBookingDialog.nextStep3Body: Just show up and let us take care of the rest.
